style_stack.visual: broll_montage PRIMARY (matrix=static plates+hard cuts; smoke keeps Ken Burns)

beats.speakable_text: If a house that echoes differently is familiar, this divorce pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun divorce usually makes the loop more expensive. More replaying the story, more explaining yourself to everyone who asks. And less room to just be in it.

This transition often persists because identity and daily structure were both built around a marriage that no longer exists. And both need rebuilding at once. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
Try this. Name one true fact about today, do the next practical task in front of you. And let the rest wait. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. The marriage ending is not the only measure of whether it mattered.
